apiVersion.versioningPolicy: No formal versioning/deprecation policy page documented. Deprecations are announced via the official changelog (https://newsdata.io/changelog); precedent: the `ai_content` response field deprecation was announced 2025-09-30 with removal effective 2025-11-01 (~1 month notice).
apiVersion.notes: Legacy endpoint /1/news is retained as a documented, fully supported alias of /1/latest.

authentication.mechanism: API key (no OAuth, no JWT documented)
authentication.rule: Send the key either as the `apikey` query-string parameter or as the `X-ACCESS-KEY` HTTP header; one of the two is required on every request. Parameter names are case-insensitive.
authentication.keyFormat: Opaque per-account string issued from the user dashboard after signup; examples show a `pub_` prefix (not guaranteed).
authentication.source: https://newsdata.io/openapi.json (securitySchemes ApiKeyQuery + ApiKeyHeader; root security requires one of the two)

baseUrls.description: Production (sole server in official OpenAPI spec); all endpoint paths are prefixed /1/, i.e. effective base https://newsdata.io/api/1
baseUrls.planSpecificHosts: None documented — single host for all plans

endpoints.name: Latest News
optionalParams: id (up to page-size limit; must be used alone)
timeframe (1-48 hours or 1m-2880m; paid plans only)
endpoints.pagination: Cursor: pass response `nextPage` as `page`; numeric page number when sort=relevancy|source|fetched_at (capped at 10,000 results)
endpoints.freshness: Rolling last-48-hours window. Real-time on paid plans; 12-hour delay on free plan. Does not accept from_date/to_date.
endpoints.notes: Filter incompatibilities: at most one of q/qInTitle/qInMeta; one of domain/domainurl/excludedomain; country XOR excludecountry; category XOR excludecategory; language XOR excludelanguage; timeframe XOR from_date/to_date; id and url must be used alone.

endpoints.name: News (legacy alias)
endpoints.notes: Documented in the OpenAPI spec as 'Identical to /1/latest — same parameters, same behaviour, same response, same 1-credit cost.'

endpoints.name: News Archive
requiredParams: At least one of: q, qInTitle, qInMeta, country, category, language, domain, domainurl, prioritydomain, id, url, image, video, full_content
endpoints.freshness: Historical (EOD-style corpus). Look-back depth is plan-gated: Basic 6 months, Professional 2 years, Corporate 10 years. Not available on the free plan.
endpoints.notes: Consumes 5 API credits per call/page — deep historical sweeps are expensive.

endpoints.name: Crypto News
optionalParams: from_date/to_date OR timeframe (paid only; not both)
endpoints.freshness: Real-time on paid plans. Free plan: last 7 days only, with 12-hour delay, and from_date/to_date/timeframe cannot be used.
endpoints.notes: No country/category filters on this endpoint. `coin` response field lists crypto ticker symbols mentioned in the article.

endpoints.name: Market (financial/stock-market) News
endpoints.freshness: Real-time on paid plans; default window last 48 hours when no dates given. Free plan: last 7 days, 12-hour delay, no date/timeframe params.
endpoints.notes: Launched 2025-10-16 per official changelog. Vendor llms.txt labels it beta; the OpenAPI spec does not. This is NEWS tagged with tickers — not price/quote data.

endpoints.name: News Sources directory
topLevelResponseFields: results[] (Source objects: id, name, url, icon, priority, description, category, language, country, total_article, last_fetch)
endpoints.pagination: None — returns up to 100 sources, nextPage is always null
endpoints.freshness: Directory data; `last_fetch` per source shows when the source was last crawled
endpoints.notes: Returned `id` is used with the `domain` filter on news endpoints.

endpoints.name: News Count
requiredParams: At least one of: q, qInTitle, qInMeta, country, category, language, domain, domainurl, prioritydomain, image, video, full_content
endpoints.freshness: Aggregates over the archive corpus; look-back bounded by plan archive depth
endpoints.notes: Paid plans only ('plans with count access'). Introduced 2025-11-20 per changelog. 50 credits per call.

endpoints.name: Crypto News Count
endpoints.notes: Paid plans only; 50 credits per call.

endpoints.name: Market News Count
endpoints.freshness: Aggregates over ticker-tagged market news corpus
endpoints.notes: Paid plans only; 50 credits per call. Useful for news-volume analytics per ticker.

coverage.assetClasses: NEWS ONLY — this provider supplies no prices, quotes, fundamentals, or reference data. News is tagged for equities (stock ticker `symbol` on /1/market) and cryptocurrencies (`coin` on /1/crypto); general/business/politics/etc. news via /1/latest and /1/archive.
coverage.geographies: 200+ countries (llms.txt cites 206), ~89 languages; filters use ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes and ISO 639-1 language codes
coverage.sources: Tens of thousands of publishers (vendor materials cite 84,000-99,000+ depending on date; not independently verifiable). Source tiers exposed via prioritydomain: top = top 10% of domains, medium = top 30%, low = top 50%.
coverage.categories: Fixed taxonomy: business, crime, domestic, education, entertainment, environment, food, health, lifestyle, other, politics, science, sports, technology, top, tourism, world
coverage.datatypes: news, blog, multimedia, forum, press release, review, research, opinion, analysis, podcast (datatype field/filter, data from 2025-11-28 onward)
coverage.chains: Not applicable — crypto coverage is by coin ticker symbol on news articles, not by blockchain/chain
coverage.instrumentTypes: Stock tickers (plain ticker strings on /1/market) and crypto ticker symbols (on /1/crypto). No ISIN/FIGI/CUSIP or exchange-qualified symbology documented.

freshness.latest: Real-time on paid plans (vendor claim); free plan articles delayed 12 hours. /1/latest covers a rolling 48-hour window.
freshness.archive: Historical corpus; depth plan-gated (6 months / 2 years / 10 years).
freshness.crypto: Real-time on paid plans; free plan limited to last 7 days with 12-hour delay.
freshness.market: Real-time on paid plans; default last-48-hours window; free plan last 7 days with 12-hour delay.
freshness.realTimeCaveat: The vendor's own Terms state: 'While reasonable efforts are made to deliver content close to the time of publication, delivery times may vary, and real-time availability is not guaranteed.' The `fetched_at` response field (added 2025-12-19) exposes collection time and enables latency measurement.
freshness.push: No webhooks/WebSocket/SSE available today; polling only. WebSocket streaming announced as 'coming soon' in the official changelog on 2026-06-23.

historicalDepth.free: No archive access; /1/latest 48h window; /1/crypto and /1/market last 7 days
historicalDepth.corporate: Archive: past 10 years (expanded per changelog entry of 2026-06-22; custom historical ranges also purchasable)
historicalDepth.enrichmentFieldDepth: AI/enrichment filters are date-gated regardless of plan: sentiment from 2024-01-12, ai_region from 2024-01-29, ai_org from 2024-05-24, removeduplicate from 2024-07-24, datatype from 2025-11-28
historicalDepth.totalCorpus: Vendor cites 100M+ articles gathered since ~2016 (marketing figure)

pagination.mechanism: Cursor-based: response field `nextPage` (opaque string, null when exhausted) is passed as the `page` query parameter on the next request with identical filters.
pagination.exception: With sort=relevancy|source|fetched_at, `page` is a page NUMBER instead, and results are capped at 10,000 total.
pagination.pageSize: size param: 1-50 on paid plans, 1-10 on free plan; defaults to plan maximum. Each page consumes the endpoint's credit cost (1 / 5 / 50).
pagination.sourcesEndpoint: /1/sources does not paginate (max 100 rows, nextPage always null)

shortWindow.paid: 1,800 credits per 15 minutes (documented uniformly for 'paid plans'; no per-tier differentiation documented)
shortWindow.onExceed: HTTP 429; docs state requests are blocked until the window resets after 15 minutes

quotas.addOnCredits: $0.006 per additional credit on paid plans (per official pricing data)

creditAccounting.articlesPerCredit: 10 on free plan, 50 on paid plans
creditAccounting.failedRequests: Docs FAQ states credits are counted only when a request executes and returns a result; requests with wrong parameters/filters or zero results are not counted

rateLimits.observabilityHeaders: Response headers `X-RateLimit-Remaining` (remaining request budget) and `X-API-Limit-Remaining` (remaining API credits) are documented in the official OpenAPI spec on 200 responses
rateLimits.backoffGuidance: No formal backoff policy in the API docs. The vendor's llms.txt recommends honoring Retry-After on 429 and exponential backoff with jitter for 429/5xx; official SDKs implement automatic retries with exponential backoff (2s->4s->8s->16s->32s, 60s cap) and honor Retry-After. Retry-After header behavior is described in vendor llms.txt, not in the OpenAPI spec.

errors.envelope: { "status": "error", "results": { "message": "<human-readable>", "code": "<ErrorCode>" } } — an InvalidFilterDetail variant adds fields such as invalid_domain, invalid_country, invalid_category, invalid_language, invalid_datatype, invalid_field, invalid_id, invalid_tag, invalid_region, invalid_coin, invalid_ticker, and a suggestion[] array

httpStatusCodes.400: BadRequest — required parameter missing or malformed query
httpStatusCodes.401: Unauthorized — missing/invalid API key or request not allowed for this key
httpStatusCodes.403: Forbidden — plan does not include this endpoint or parameter
httpStatusCodes.422: UnprocessableEntity — unsupported filter value, too many values, invalid date, or incompatible filter combination
httpStatusCodes.429: TooManyRequests — rate limit exceeded or API credits exhausted
httpStatusCodes.503: ServiceUnavailable — endpoint temporarily unavailable due to maintenance

machineReadableCodes: ServiceUnavailable (NB: the enum value contains a trailing space, per the official spec)

free.endpoints: latest, news, crypto (7-day window), market (7-day window), sources
free.excluded: archive, all count endpoints, full content, timeframe param, sentiment/AI filters, real-time (12-hour delay applies)

basic.adds: Real-time delivery, archive (6 months), count endpoints, full content, article images, timeframe, AI summary, tracker/advanced search/save search/data download (dashboard), add-on credits at $0.006
basic.excluded: sentiment analysis, AI tags, ai_region/ai_org filters

professional.adds: Sentiment analysis + sentiment_score filter, AI tags (tag filter), archive depth 2 years, dedicated support, AI content
professional.excluded: ai_region filter (Corporate only); organization filter documented as Corporate-only in OpenAPI

corporate.adds: Archive depth 10 years, ai_region + organization (ai_org) filters, all AI features, dedicated support

entitlements.customEnterprise: Custom pricing via sales; unlimited/negotiated credits; custom historical data ranges purchasable (also one-off historical downloads via https://newsdata.io/historical-news-download)
entitlements.parameterGating: OpenAPI documents per-parameter gating: timeframe = paid plans only; sentiment/sentiment_score/tag = Professional+Corporate; region/organization = Corporate; full_content = 'plans with full-content access'; archive/count = 'plans with archive/count access'. Filter-value caps: 'up to your plan's filter limit (default 5)'.

sdks.status: Official; vendor llms.txt documents Python 3.8-3.14 support, retries with backoff, Retry-After handling, pagination helpers (scroll/paginate), typed exceptions
Official MCP server exposing latest/archive/crypto/market endpoints as AI-assistant tools

sdks.repo: not found on official GitHub org listing
sdks.status: Referenced only in vendor marketing (llms.txt); NOT verified as existing official SDKs — treat as unknown

licensing.grant: Terms grant a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sub-licensable license to use the API for personal or internal business purposes; a separate clause grants use of 'Data Derivatives' for internal AND external business purposes.
licensing.redistribution: Terms explicitly state: 'The customers can sublicense, sell, syndicate or otherwise share the Data with any third party on their own risk.' NewsData.io simultaneously disclaims ownership: article content 'remains the sole property of those who make it available' and 'NewsData.io is a data aggregator and doesn't have the right to authorise any user to use the data for their personal and commercial purposes' — i.e., redistribution/republication risk (copyright) sits entirely with the customer.
licensing.storageCaching: No storage or caching restriction is documented in the Terms; vendor guidance (llms.txt best practices) actively recommends server-side caching. Long-term archival storage is not explicitly addressed — not documented.
licensing.attribution: No mandatory attribution/display requirement in the Terms. An optional mutual trademark license permits displaying the NewsData.io icon/advertising usage. Vendor best-practice guidance recommends displaying source_name and linking back to the original article.
licensing.prohibitions: May not copy/rent/lease/sell/transfer/sublicense/reverse-engineer the API itself ('this may not be applicable to the data returned by the NewsData.io API'); may not share API keys; may not make the API available over a network for use by others; may not bypass the API-key registration process.
licensing.warranties: Content provided as-is; NewsData.io disclaims accuracy/completeness/availability warranties and does not guarantee real-time delivery. DMCA takedown process documented; content may be removed at any time.
licensing.governingLaw: India (exclusive jurisdiction of Indian courts). Operating entity referenced in Terms as NewsData.io/Bytesview; third-party directories cited by vendor llms.txt name Algodom Media LLP.
licensing.freeDatasets: Separate free curated datasets on GitHub are CC BY 4.0 (attribution required) — distinct from API data.
licensing.commercialUseFreeTier: Vendor FAQ states commercial use is permitted on the free plan.

limitations: News-only provider: no prices, quotes, candles, fundamentals, filings, or reference data. The 'Market' endpoint is ticker-tagged news, not market data.
Free tier articles are delayed 12 hours and crypto/market lookback is 7 days — free-tier output must never be labeled real-time.
Terms explicitly do not guarantee real-time delivery even on paid plans; use the fetched_at field to measure actual collection latency.

No push delivery (webhooks/WebSocket/SSE) as of the review date; polling only, bounded by 1,800 credits/15min on paid plans. WebSocket streaming announced 'coming soon' (changelog 2026-06-23) but not yet documented as available.

Symbol mapping is bare ticker strings (e.g. AMZN, BTC) with no exchange qualification, ISIN/FIGI/CUSIP, or documented symbology standard — ticker collisions across exchanges are unaddressed; institutional symbol mapping must be done downstream.

AI enrichment (sentiment, tags, region, org) is vendor-model output with no documented accuracy metrics, and is date-gated (sentiment only from 2024-01-12, ai_org from 2024-05-24, datatype from 2025-11-28) — historical backfills of these fields are incomplete.
Archive depth is plan-gated (6mo/2y/10y) and archive calls cost 5 credits per page; count calls cost 50 credits — historical corpus building at scale is credit-expensive.
sort=relevancy|source|fetched_at result sets are hard-capped at 10,000 results.
Market endpoint is recent (launched 2025-10-16) and labeled beta in vendor materials; schema/behavior may change.

Rate-limit numbers (30/1,800 per 15 min) appear in docs-page text and vendor llms.txt but not in the OpenAPI spec; Retry-After semantics are described only in the vendor llms.txt/SDKs — not formally specified.

Internal inconsistencies between official sources: llms.txt says filter-value caps rise to 10 on Pro/Corporate while the OpenAPI spec says 'default 5' for all plans and pricing data gives q-length 100/100/256/512 while llms.txt says 100 across free — verify caps with vendor before capacity planning.
No public SLA or uptime commitment documented (pricing feature matrix has an 'uptime' boolean for paid tiers with no percentage defined).
Publisher-content copyright remains with original publishers; redistribution/derivative display of full content is at the customer's own legal risk (Terms).
'duplicate' flag and removeduplicate filter exist, but dedup quality is undocumented; content field may be truncated for some publishers even on paid plans due to upstream restrictions.
Docs site is a client-side React SPA (not crawlable server-side); authoritative machine-readable spec is https://newsdata.io/openapi.json.
Governing law/jurisdiction is India; entity is an LLP — relevant for institutional vendor-risk review.

primaryFor: Broad multi-geography, multi-language news monitoring and aggregation (200+ countries, ~89 languages, source-tier filtering)
Crypto news feed with per-coin tagging and vendor sentiment (Professional+ for sentiment filter)
Ticker-tagged equity news flow and news-volume analytics (market + market/count endpoints) where seconds-level latency is not required
Historical news corpus construction for NLP/backtesting research (Corporate: 10-year archive; one-off historical downloads available)
fallbackFor: Market-moving real-time news alerts — pull-only polling, uncommitted latency, and no SLA make it a fallback behind institutional low-latency news feeds
Sentiment signal generation — vendor AI sentiment is coarse (3-way + stats) with no documented accuracy; use as a secondary/confirmation signal
notSuitableFor: Compliance-grade audit trails of publisher content (content may be removed at any time; no completeness warranty)
Sub-minute latency trading triggers (no push delivery; polling + credit windows)
hardConstraints: Free-tier (12-hour delayed) data must never be presented as real-time; even paid 'real-time' is contractually best-effort — display fetched_at-derived latency where freshness matters
Full-content republication requires downstream copyright clearance — Terms place all redistribution risk on the customer; prefer excerpt + source_name + link-back
Credit budget must reserve 5x for archive pages and 50x for count calls; per-15-minute ceiling (1,800 credits paid) bounds peak ingestion at ~90,000 articles/15min
Plan gating must be enforced in the adapter: archive/count/timeframe/full_content/sentiment/tag (Pro+), region/organization (Corporate)
Symbol/coin tags must be mapped to internal security master before joining with market data

sourcesConsulted.whatItVerified: Official OpenAPI 3.1.0 spec (info.version 1.0.0): server https://newsdata.io/api; both auth schemes (apikey query param, X-ACCESS-KEY header); all 9 GET endpoints with full parameter definitions, plan/date gating notes, credit costs; response schemas (Article/CryptoArticle/MarketArticle/Source/Count envelopes); error envelope + full ErrorCode enum; HTTP status codes 200/400/401/403/422/429/500/503; pagination rules incl. 10,000-result cap on relevancy/source/fetched_at sorts; X-RateLimit-Remaining and X-API-Limit-Remaining response headers; filter incompatibility rules; free-plan 12-hour delay and 7-day crypto/market windows.

Vendor-published LLM knowledge file (compiled June 2026): rate limits (30 credits/15min free, 1,800/15min paid, 200/day free), monthly credit allocations, plan prices, articles-per-credit (10 free/50 paid), coverage claims (200+ countries, ~89 languages, sources counts), category/datatype taxonomies, SDK feature detail, Retry-After/backoff guidance, no-webhook status, best practices, known limitations. Self-flags some items as [UNVERIFIED]; used as corroboration, not sole source, where possible.

Live data source that renders the official /pricing page: per-plan API credits (200/day, 20k, 50k, 1M per month), articles per credit (10/50/50/50), USD monthly/yearly prices (0, 199.99/1919.99, 349.99/3359.99, 1299.99/9989.99), add-on credit price $0.006, feature matrix (archive false on free; count_api false on free; ai_tags and sentiment_analysis Professional+; ai_region Corporate only; full_content/timeframe/real-time paid only; historical_data '6 months'/'2 Year'/'10 Years'), q_limit per plan (100/100/256/512).

Official changelog data (renders /changelog): WebSocket streaming 'coming soon' (2026-06-23); Corporate plan extended to 10 years historical + custom historical ranges (2026-06-22); official Postman collection (2025-12-29); datatype/fetched_at/sort updates (2025-12-19); datatype field (2025-11-28); News Count API launch (2025-11-20); market-endpoint AI tags (2025-11-12); excludecountry (2025-11-11); Market endpoint launch (2025-10-16); ai_content field deprecation effective 2025-11-01 announced 2025-09-30 (deprecation-practice evidence).

Canonical docs root (client-side React SPA; confirmed via page fetch + bundle). Content verified from its production asset https://s.newsdata.io/assets/DocumentationPage-Cjh09Tba.js: rate-limit text ('Free Plan: 30 credits every 15 minutes', 'Paid plans: 1800 credits every 15 minutes', 429 until window resets), credit-consumption rules (archive 5 credits, count 50 credits), free-plan article delay statement, timeframe semantics (1-48h, 1m-2880m), archive plan-gating, pubDateTZ field, 'Real-time WebSocket (Coming Soon)' section.

Terms & Conditions (content verified from production asset https://s.newsdata.io/assets/TermsConditions-CjW6fGAk.js): license grant scope, API-key confidentiality, prohibited uses, data-ownership disclaimers, customer right to 'sublicense, sell, syndicate or otherwise share the Data... on their own risk', no real-time guarantee clause, optional trademark/attribution license, as-is warranty disclaimer, DMCA process, India governing law, refund/cancellation terms.

Official GitHub organization ('Official NewsData.io repositories for SDKs...'): confirmed official SDKs — python-client, php-client, newsdata-nodejs-client, newsdata-reactjs-client, newsdata-java-sdk, and newsdata.io-mcp (official MCP server). No .NET/Go/Dart repos observed in the pinned/listed set.

Official pricing page URL (SPA; renders from /web/v1/pricing above). Pricing FAQ text confirmed from asset https://s.newsdata.io/assets/Pricing-BF77I1Ty.js: API-credit definition (only counted when executed with results; wrong parameters/zero results not counted), archive = 5 credits regardless of filters, monthly/yearly billing.

openQuestions: Per-plan filter-value caps: OpenAPI says 'default 5' for all list filters; vendor llms.txt says 10 on Professional/Corporate — needs vendor confirmation.
Search-query max length: pricing data q_limit says 100/100/256/512 by plan; llms.txt says 100-char cap on free — the OpenAPI 'default 100' is consistent, but per-plan values should be confirmed.
Is the 1,800 credits/15min ceiling identical for Corporate/Enterprise, and is it negotiable?
Is /1/market still formally beta (llms.txt says beta; OpenAPI and changelog do not flag it)?
SLA/uptime: pricing matrix has an 'uptime' boolean for paid tiers but no documented percentage or SLA terms.
Retry-After header on 429: described in vendor llms.txt and honored by SDKs, but not present in the OpenAPI spec — confirm it is actually emitted.
Existence/maintenance of .NET, Go, and Dart/Flutter SDKs (marketing mention only; not on the GitHub org listing).
WebSocket streaming GA date, pricing, and plan availability (announced 'coming soon' 2026-06-23).
Explicit contractual position on long-term storage/archival of retrieved articles (Terms are silent; caching is recommended in vendor guidance).
Legal operating entity confirmation (Algodom Media LLP vs 'NewsData.io/Bytesview' as named in Terms) for vendor due diligence.
